attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-GI-03-00210 — SEQ is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-GI-03 bucket.
Age (~42) inferred — no age stated in the source note; please confirm on clinician review.
Sex_at_birth (female) inferred — no sex stated in the source note; coeliac has a modest female predominance, but this is a guess for a required enum. Confirm on review.
Geographic context (RA1_major_city) inferred from 'apartment/flat' — confirm on review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source note is an in-person home visit where the clinician percussed/auscultated/palpated the abdomen to exclude a surgical cause and manage at home. The AI Doctor cannot examine, so the correct-answer triage was raised to urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) with surgical red-flag safety-netting, rather than the source's benign home-management conclusion. The clinician-only exam findings and vitals are sealed in nodes 10/11.

No specific dose was stated in the source for oral hyoscine butylbromide, so dose_route_frequency was omitted (no dose synthesised); it is scoped as an acceptable symptomatic option only after in-person exclusion of a surgical abdomen.

Clinician-measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 92, RR 18, SpO2 99%) were treated as clinician-obtained and sealed/omitted rather than presented as patient-obtainable; only a home-thermometer temperature and self-reported weight were offered in 01.objective_data_offered.
All SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes are candidates pending terminology-server verification.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
